activities_projects.title: Causes of the Revolt Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the major causes of the Revolt of 1857
steps: List political, economic and military causes of the revolt
Explain how these causes affected soldiers and rulers
Write which cause you think was most important and why
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of historical causes

activities_projects.title: Leaders of the Revolt Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of key leaders in the revolt
steps: Choose two leaders such as Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib or Bahadur Shah Zafar
Write their role in the revolt
Explain how they contributed to the resistance against British rule

activities_projects.title: Sepoy Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Understand the revolt from the viewpoint of soldiers
steps: Imagine you are a sepoy in 1857
Write a diary entry describing your problems with British policies
Explain why you decided to join the revolt

activities_projects.title: Map Study of the Revolt
activities_projects.objective: Identify important centres of the Revolt of 1857
steps: Mark important centres such as Meerut, Delhi, Kanpur and Jhansi
Write one event related to each place
Explain why these places were important during the revolt
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands geographical spread of the revolt

activities_projects.title: British Response Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the British reacted to the revolt
steps: Write how the British suppressed the revolt
Explain the steps taken after the revolt to control India
Write one major administrative change introduced

activities_projects.title: Source Interpretation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Interpret historical sources related to the revolt
steps: Read a source or extract related to the revolt
Explain what the source tells about the event
Discuss whether the source may show bias
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops skills in analysing historical sources

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Revolt Participants
activities_projects.objective: Compare different groups involved in the revolt
steps: Write the role of sepoys in the revolt
Write the role of peasants or rulers
Compare how their reasons for joining the revolt were different

activities_projects.title: Visual Source Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Interpret historical illustrations from the chapter
steps: Observe a picture related to the Revolt of 1857
Describe the scene shown
Explain what the picture reveals about the revolt
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Improves skills in visual historical interpretation

activities_projects.title: Cause–Effect Chart of the Revolt
activities_projects.objective: Understand relationships between causes and results
steps: Write major causes of the revolt
Write immediate effects after the revolt
Connect the causes and effects with arrows

activities_projects.title: Mini Research on the Revolt of 1857
activities_projects.objective: Encourage deeper research on the revolt
steps: Choose one leader or event from the revolt
Collect information from at least two sources
Write a short report summarising your findings
